Quick Facts
  • Sabahudin Tricic was born and grew up in Rudo, Bosnia. He started practicing Karate at the age of 15 in a local school club. He trained there for two years, and was invited to Karate Club Priboj, where he practiced and competed in local tournaments in Kumite for another two years. Upon enrolling in college in Sarajevo, Bosnia, Sabahudin was invited to join Karate Club “Bosna,” university club at Faculty of Sarajevo, where he competed as U-21. There, he made the senior representation team, by winning the first place in National Championship in -75kg Elite Kumite, and third place in Elite Team Kumite at Balkan Championship. However, Sabahudin’s Kumite career was cut short by war in 1992, when he was blinded by a shrapnel
  • Tricic plays the accordion and fishes.
Career Highlights
  • Sabahudin was evacuated to USA for medical treatment, where he continued his Karate career, training his children at the same time. He has competed in numerous local tournaments in Kata and won medals in a regular sighted category. Sabahudin has been a National champion in 18+ Male Elite Para-Kata Visually Impaired category six times, and PanAmerican Champion twice. At World Senior Championships, Sabahudin has placed 6th twice and 5th once. Sabahudin has also hosted the Bosnian team for USA Open Championship three times, providing the opportunity to youth born during the war to compete on an international level. Sabahudin is a 4th Dan in Shotokan and a brown belt in Yamani Ryu Kobudo and Daito Ryu Aiki Jitsu.
  • PKF: 2017 1st place, Curacao
  • PKF: 2018 2nd place, Santiago, Chile
  • PKF: 2019 1st place, Panama City, Panama
  • 6x National Championships Gold Medalist
  • 2x National Championships Silver Medalist
  • 1x USA Open Championships Gold Medalist
  • 1x USA Open Championships Silver Medalist
  • 3x USA Open Championships Bronze Medalist

Quick Facts
  • Position: Forward
  • Son of Rita and Vincent, Sr. Trocheck
Biographical Information

Vincent Trocheck grew up in Pittsburgh where he played youth hockey with future U.S. and New York Rangers teammate J.T. Miller.  

After playing junior hockey in Michigan, Trocheck was a third-round pick in the 2011 NHL Draft by the Florida Panthers. Trocheck made IIHF World Championship debut with the U.S. men's hockey team in 2014. He would return to the men's senior national team over a decade later when he was named to the 2025 4 Nations Face-Off roster. 

Trocheck’s grandmother emigrated to the U.S. from Calabria, Italy and Sunday family dinners have been a tradition for Trocheck since he was a child. Trocheck's grandmother has hosted even his NHL teammates for a Sunday family dinner when his teams are in town visiting Pittsburgh for a game.

  • Top finish: 6th – 2014 (Team - Men)

    • Olympic Games Milan 2026, gold (Team - Men)

Quick Facts

Quick Facts

Horses have always been a part of Roxanne “Roxie” Trunnell’s life. As a competitor in able-bodied dressage, Trunnell aspired to be an Olympian. When she was a teenager, she created her own business to help purchase her first dressage horse, Nice Touch or ‘Touché.' She earned a United States Dressage Federation (USDF) bronze medal and was close to obtaining a silver medal until contracting a virus in 2009 that caused swelling in her brain that changed her life forever. The virus put her in a coma and resulted in her requiring a wheelchair. Determined to ride Touché again, Trunnell elicited the help of her family and friends to get her back in the saddle and after a long recovery, slowly began to ride again. Sher entered the Tokyo Games as the highest-ranked Para Dressage athlete in the world, per the FEI Para Dressage World Individual Ranking. Additionally, Trunnell completed her Masters in Psychology with a focus in Equine-Assisted Psychotherapy.

    • Paralympic Games Tokyo 2020, gold (Individual Championship Test - Grade I - Open, Individual Freestyle - Grade I - Open), bronze (Team - Open)
    • Paralympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, 10th (Individual Championship Test - Grade Ia - Open)
